label: New — Today's Calendar on phones
items: Tap the Today tile on your mobile Quick Access screen for a new single-page view: when to plant what, whether you need to water, and your tasks for the day — anchored to your home's local frost dates.

Type any plant name (tomato, garlic, sunflower…) and Rhozly's AI tells you the earliest and latest outdoor planting dates for your climate, whether to start indoors first, spacing, depth, sun, and a few tips. Backed by your home's last and first frost dates, looked up once and cached for six months.
Frost-date lookup is open to all tiers — the cached result is treated as a fact. Per-plant planting guidance needs an AI tier (Sage or Evergreen).

label: New — Rain advice tile
items: A new tile on the Today screen synthesises today + tomorrow's rainfall against your open watering tasks. Four verdicts: skip watering, rain's got it covered, water today, or just an informational mm count.

Both thresholds (skip ≥ N mm, water < N mm) are now editable per home in Account → Home Management → Settings → Rain advice. Defaults are 5 mm and 1 mm — the same numbers Rhozly's weather rules already use.

label: Behind the scenes
items: Today's tasks on the Quick Access calendar use the same write paths and completion flow as the full Dashboard — no duplicated task code.
Server-side validation now refuses to cache frost dates that fall outside the plausible months for your hemisphere, so you'll never see a 'last spring frost in October' answer in the UK.
